persona: A 35-year-old graphic designer who works from home

expanded_persona.base_persona: A 35-year-old graphic designer who works from home
work_education: Freelance graphic designer specializing in brand identities
family_relationships: Lives alone with two cats named Pixel and Vector

work_habits: Always uses dark mode in every application
Prefers to work in complete silence — no music, no podcasts
health_body: Severely allergic to shellfish — cannot eat shrimp, crab, or lobster
Drinks exactly two cups of green tea every morning, never coffee

life_event.event: Set up a new home office

conversation.content: Hi! I'm a graphic designer and I just set up my new home office. I always use dark mode in every application — it's easier on my eyes since I stare at screens all day.
Dark mode is great for reducing eye strain during long design sessions. Have you also considered adjusting your monitor's color temperature?
Good idea. Also, I should mention I have a severe shellfish allergy — no shrimp, crab, or lobster for me. It's important because I sometimes order lunch delivery while working.
That's important to know. When ordering delivery, you might want to add allergy notes to your profile on food delivery apps so restaurants are aware.
I prefer to work in complete silence — no music, no podcasts, nothing. And I drink exactly two cups of green tea every morning, never coffee. That's my whole routine.
Sounds like a focused routine. Green tea gives a gentle caffeine boost without the jitters, and silence helps maintain deep concentration for design work.

life_event.event: Started sharing office with partner who plays music

from.fact: Prefers to work in complete silence — no music, no podcasts

to.fact: Now wears noise-canceling headphones with white noise since partner plays music in the shared office

evolved.trigger: Partner moved into the home office

conversation.content: My partner moved into my home office and plays music all day. I used to work in complete silence, but now I've switched to wearing noise-canceling headphones with white noise to block it out.
That's a practical adaptation. White noise can be even better than silence for maintaining focus, especially in a shared space.
Yeah, it's working well. Everything else is the same — still on dark mode, still drinking my two cups of green tea in the morning, and definitely still avoiding shellfish.
Good to hear the core routine is holding up. The headphones seem like a solid solution for the shared office situation.
